mission.title: alertmanager: Jira Integration deprecated Endpoints
mission.description: Jira Integration deprecated Endpoints. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify alertmanager upgrade symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your alertmanager deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager -n alertmanager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review alertmanager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager
```
Creating a Jira Ticket via the Jira Integration resulting in a HTTP 401 followed by a deprecation

steps.title: Apply the fix for Jira Integration deprecated Endpoints
steps.description:
After a [chat](https://cloud-native.slack.com/archives/C01AUBA4PFE/p1757006154523549) with , I would like to share a proposal to solve this issue:

### Proposal

Introduce a new configuration toggle `api_type` to control which Jira API the notifier uses.

### Motivation

Currently, the notifier relies on a single code path that supports Jira Data Center APIs and Jira Cloud APIs which are deprecated. This causes problems for Jira Cloud users, since newer endpoints are required for issue

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Jira Integration deprecated Endpoints is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager -n alertmanager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
After a [chat](https://cloud-native.slack.com/archives/C01AUBA4PFE/p1757006154523549) with , I would like to share a proposal to solve this issue:

### Proposal

Introduce a new configuration toggle `api_type` to control which Jira API the notifier uses.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
